reviewDescription: Horrendous experience. My rep repeatedly failed to return emails. When I called, he would not be available. When I set up a callback, his schedule was always full (for all times for months). When I left a message to call me, he'd sometimes not. And, most of all the valuation team required a roof replacement before funding the loan in W.Pa in January. My inspection said the roof was 20 yrs old, but sound. My agreement included escrow for roof replacement at closing. There was NO reason to require replacement before closing. Temp was consistently in the teens and 20s, but I needed to get a roofer to do the job now or no loan. My sales agreement said the roof replacement at escrow all along my process, but Better waited until the day before closing to say the roof MUST be replaced now. It delayed closing by 3 weeks. My seller had breast cancer getting radiation daily and lived in a hotel for those 3 weeks because she had emptied the house. There was no one who would listen to reason or logic. My rep said, "it's those other people." Our appraiser called them and said, "tell me where my appraisal says the roof is unsound and I'll take it out." Yet, they kept calling it "unliveable," when it simply was not. It is this kind of dangerous to enter into business with a company that has no logic, no reason, no integrity, and no empathy. BBB says Better gets 1.9 out of 5 for customer service, and you have to give a one - like here.
reviewCompanyResponse:
Tim,
Thank you for bringing these concerns regarding your experience to our attention. We are committed to making homeownership simpler, faster, and more accessible for our customers and I am so sorry to hear this has not been the case for you. We appreciate your honest feedback and we will be connecting the appropriate leaders to research the defects you outlined. Our Client Success Team would love to serve as an advocate to identify any potential paths forward. We can be reached by email at clientsatisfaction@better.com.

reviewTitle: Great experience and to the point.
reviewDescription:
Great experience and to the point.
I really enjoyed working with my Better agent, Sirague, when I needed to. The rest of the time the website was very resourceful. Overall there was very good active communication, and I never got the feeling that I was being sold something etc. The competitors tried really hard to differentiate themselves and in some cases pointed to recent events with Better CEO, but the bottom line is the rate was unbeatable. Competitors did have really good bedside manner though, but I still had the feeling they were trying to sell me something. So I locked with Better, and when my credit improved substantially, Sirague was able to update terms with no fuss. When issues came up due to title company having their own non-industry standard process, Sirague was able to communicate effectively and guide me towards a constructive solution for all parties. When I was brainstorming an appeal to appraisal, he was  informative, transparent, and supportive. (I cancelled appeal however as I negotiated a credit to do so with seller.) I would definitely go with Better again, rates are very competitive and the agents appear to be very well trained.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hi there, thank you for leaving your review and thank you for sticking with Better throughout this process. We are so glad we could provide a satisfactory rate and your loan team was able to help you navigate through closing. We would love to work with you again in the future and hope you have a wonderful year ahead.

reviewTitle: If you are computer savvy or even not Better…
reviewDescription: If you are computer savvy or even not then Better Mortgage might be the Mortgage Company for you.  You have a dashboard  online where you see all of your tasks such as uploading w2 and income verification.  There is no office to drop and there is no dedicated loan officer.  You do get a Home Advisor who guides you on your way through emails and phone calls you can schedule.  Ankit my Home Advisor kept in touch with me through emails and he was great! You also have a dedicated customer service team who will try to answer you questions.  There is no Origination Fees unless you are paying with mortgage points. Because of this they are able to offer you a Better Mortgage (Yes Pun intended) and a Fantastic interest rate!  As a consumer I was in contact with another mortgage company and they offered me a significant higher interest rate and also Origination Fees.  When I went to closing, everyone at the table was astonished of my interest rate.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thanks so much for sharing the details of your experience here. We are so happy we could help you meet your financial goals. If there is anything we can do to assist you in the future, please do not hesitate to reach us at hello@better.com!

reviewTitle: My experience with Better.com is very positive
reviewDescription: My experience with Better.com is very positive. I was offered with different rates day by day during the initial days when I was searching for better rates and my loan advisor, Evelyn was in touch with me to help me decide my priorities and timeline. Good thing is all of these were gentle reminders and helped me in informed decision making. Even after locking the rate and till closing, online platform was easy to navigate around, with overview of loan stage, tasks that needed to be completed. Uploading documents was easy and review was fast.  During each stage of loan, all my questions were answered by my loan advisor and her team, which made me feel at ease working with them. My home CO was delayed till the last moment acceptable, but Better was still able to process my loan. They were also fast to accommodate my requests and communicate with title company regarding my requests. Only thing we noted and title company pointed out was stated property taxes on escrow were almost double the property tax in the county. I am hoping to see then correct those within the next 1 year.  Finally I am looking forward for good service from their mortgage loan servicer.

reviewTitle: I had a great experience with Better…
reviewDescription: I had a great experience with Better throughout the whole process. Great customer service!

Chris was great, he kept following up with every update which I did appreciate. Other than the closing lady that came to my house 2 hours late, everything went great and it was over all pretty easy as long as you keep up with all your tasks in the online process.

Wonderful experience overall! When I encountered some roadblocks outside Betters control and felt defeated, my home advisor and team came through in spades and crossed the hurdles through a smooth closing.
Faith was very helpful.  Would recommend her.

My wife and I were extremely pleased with our Better refinancing experience.  The process takes place totally online, with user-friendly webpages and prompts.  The company even sent a notary to our home so we wouldn't have to venture into COVID World to sign the closing documents.  Our Home Advisor, Johny Martinez, was respectful, knowledgeable, and professional in every way.  He seemed to be available around the clock via text to answer our questions, and was intimately familiar with the ins and outs of how the Better team does  mortgage refinancing.  My one disappointment was the prompts we received by email.  Some were a bit unclear (e.g., I got repeated prompts to e-sign a document, which I kept following puzzlingly to no avail until Johny explained that my wife had to sign, too.)  And some prompts weren't exactly...prompt.  For instance, the day after we closed on the loan we got a prompt saying we had five remaining tasks to complete before we could close.  All in all, however, our entire experience was exceptionally easy and flexible.  While Better is a business that's trying to make money just like every other lender, I felt that their entire process was geared to the borrower's interests, concerns, and needs.  So it's not hard for me to understand why so many consumers rate them so highly.

reviewDescription:

He was extremely pushy with paperwork when I was clear from the beginning I was still in the process of comparing rates between mortgage companies. When I asked him not to be so pushy he just said that he wasn’t and continued to do too much. Gaslighting customers?? 

When I had to keep going back and forth with rates Brendan was getting upset that we were doing so much back and forth and wanted me to just move forward with Better. I explained again I had to continue to compare rates and each company kept beating the next. If mortgage companies just provided the actual best rate the first time we wouldn’t have to do this annoying dance. He works for a mortgage company so he knows the game, but continued to be pushy.

As soon as I officially decided I no longer wanted to use Better, that is when all of a sudden he went from blowing up my phone and e-mail to no where to be found. I was able to finally have the refund processed. 

Overall, not a good experience with Better.

The sole positive note in refinancing my current mortgage through Better.com was the ability to accomplish this primarily electronically.  However, this did not result in a streamlined experience, nor more timely refinancing.  Information is apparently distributed through multiple departments which fail to communicate, requiring multiple interventions on my part.  More significantly, the scheduled closing date was delayed without notification.  Finally, transfer of servicing remains a bit of a mess.

reviewDescription: Don't let recent scandals with the CEO cause you to judge this company, process or people.  I almost did out of sheer principle.  I am VERY happy I didn't.  This company is the PERFECT blend of tech, process, and outstanding PEOPLE, to turn a confusing, convoluted process like mortgage finance into a simple, painless process.  Sure the tech is cool and does help, but it is the knowledge, professionalism, and genuine caring of the PEOPLE that differentiate this company from every other in this market space.  I will choose no other moving forward.

I would like to express my concerns about the recent experience I had with Better Mortgage.   I have used Better Mortgage now for two transactions, a refinancing of my current home and a purchase of a new construction home.  The refinance was an incredibly easy and smooth experience and left me thinking how easy the online portal for Better Mortgage was to use. There was great communication throughout.  
As a result, I decided to go with Better Mortgage for a new construction home. I had already received a pre approval letter from the builders finance company but considered Better Mortgage based on my last experience. 
This to me now was a mistake. The experience was mostly disappointing.  
I want to first off commend Johnathon and Cole for their outstanding efforts and incredible communication throughout the process.  They were the best part of the experience.   Johnathon was always available to me and his communication via text and telephone was professional and decisive. 
 My complaints stem mostly with my experience with Annika and the cost of my loan.  

My interactions with Annika were informative and friendly.  The problem stems from the fact that they were inconsistent and that she was practically impossible to reach at times where I really needed to speak or text with her.  On the day before my loan closing, I had some concerns with the sudden change in closing costs and added fees to the loan.  We spend time going over this and she promised me she would look into it and let me know in the morning before closing.  When I didn’t hear from her, I called Better Mortgage and was told she was off work that day I was told to speak to Johnathon. A simple communication would have saved me time and effort in trying to reach out to her regarding a discrepancy on my loan.  Johnathon was great but he did not have any information on the previous evenings conversation with Annika and was put in a very precarious position.  I can only presume that Annika’s actions are based on any or all of the following:  that she is overworked, unorganized, not committed to a customer-oriented approach to her trade or simply unable to perform the most important part of this position which in my opinion is being available to the client and answering questions during a stressful period for most clients.  

The other major issue I had with this experience is the loan itself.  My initial loan disclosure as well as ALL subsequent loan disclosures had several credits outlined on them.  One of these credits was for over $4000.00.   This brought my cash to close to about 10k.   The day before I was closing this credit was removed and I was told that the builder refused to provide this credit.  Why was this not researched prior to this?  The process had been going on for several months and yet the team that was working on my loan researched every aspect of the loan and my qualification for the loan.  And yet they were unable to confirm this credit with the builder.   This to me seemed like a bait and switch tactic to make this loan appear more desirable than it really was.  The day of closing I had many questions about the loan that Annika had committed to answer and she was unavailable.  Johnathon was unable to answer to no fault of his own.  

I don’t need to go on about how I won’t be referring Better mortgage to other people and so on as we all know how social media and community recommendations are a valuable tool and selecting services.  However,  I would like some acknowledgment and or compensation from Better Mortgage to make things right.  I should never have been put in that position.  Buying a new home is supposed to be a positive experience, especially if it’s the first time.  My experience was completely tarnished as I had to scramble to find five thousand extra dollars the day of closing.  

I look forward to hearing from you regarding this disappointing experience. 

Moyez Charania. 
Loan Number : 1987541528

The other variable I would change is I would've preferred to have a team that was either on CST or EST.  That would've aligned better for us.

reviewDescription: Our experience was great! Everyone was knowledgeable, answered all of our questions, and the process was so fast! We got a great rate refinancing down to a 15 year fixed and even got some money back!

My Better.com agent Adan, was really wonderful. I'm not the most tech-savvy person in the world and he worked with me to get me through to the finish line.
The closing was super easy and overall the experience was better than I had with the more traditional way of buying or refinancing a house.

I was disappointed from the beginning. I was looking to buy around the border of two states.  The first realtor that called me failed to tell me that she was only licensed in one of those states so she was only sending me properties in the state she was licensed in and most of them were not even in the towns I told her I was interested in.  Which left me to do a lot of the leg work myself.  When I sent her a property I wanted to look at in the state she was not licensed in, she then partnered up with a realtor licensed in the other state. At first he made me feel like he cared about finding me the right home until it took longer than we thought.  He told me that he felt I was worried about how much of his time it took for him to come out and show me all the properties I saw and that I shouldn't worry about that. He obviously felt I took too much of his time.  Once I found a house and it came down to inspections, he recommended an inspector for me to use.  Never advised that I should get the well and septic inspected.  I asked about the well inspection only because a friend mentioned it to me and he replied "definitely good idea".  I also found it interesting that the only time the realtor was at a house before I arrived was the day of the inspection.  The inspector took a sample of the water and never let me know how the test came back, showed my pics of the well pipes when I was at the house but they are not in the final report I received. Missed a 14" long crack in one of the pipes in the crawl that was found after I moved into the house and my septic pump went out. The septic alarm was on silence mode, which I would think would be something an inspector would have noticed and mentioned. As for the HA I did not feel confident that he knew what he was doing.  They requested things from me that they had and I didn't due to the cash offer program I used (which was not explained to me in full detail).  I felt like they requested things at the last minute then made me feel like it was my fault I didn't get it to them in a timely manner. I was told one thing from the realtor and another from the HA.  My closing disclosure was sent to me incorrect multiple times. At one point my HA said I wasn't singing it I just needed to check the box that I received it and he would get me a corrected one ASAP.  I had to tell them what was missing or incorrect on the disclosure. I didn't receive a correct one until the night before my signing.  The title company contacted me to confirm my signing appointment and asked if I had my final disclosure and stated that they had not heard from my lender. I can keep going but what it boils down to is that I will not be recommending Better Mortgage or Better Real-estate to any of my friends or family.

reviewTitle: Thumbs up to Online Application & E-Sign
reviewDescription:
I really liked the ease of the online application process and the fact that I had one main person to contact during my refi process. Landon was great! Also, the supporting personnel I contacted through the Help button were all very courteous. The reason I don't grant a 5 star is the hassle I went through clearing the title because of HELOC confusion. Someone wasn't looking at my uploaded documentation carefully enough, so the closing was slower than I thought it would be and I was getting quite frustrated.

When I finally DID get to the closing part, it was great being able to review all the documents that I was going to sign online (and then e-sign) before the actual closing appointment. Plus, the notary (Jonathan) selected for my closing was professional, knowledgeable and personable. Lastly, the funding came through as I was told it would. Thank you, Better.com!

Entire refi process was extremely simple, support was always available and very responsive. Website provided all possible mortgage options from paying points to a no cost loan and clearly detailed costs, fees and payment amounts.

I had the privilege of working with Taylor, he was professional, polite and most importantly not pushy. He even explained why his offer was better than competitors. I felt like I was at used car dealership with the other guys(rocket mortgage, loandepot) . They gave me the whole I've been in the business a long time ,this is the best you're gonna do routine. Taylor was the polar opposite

The process was quick and easy,I just don't like that they don't service the loans, you don't know who you are gonna end up with. My loan ended getting serviced by the Money source, I hope their app and website work well.

I have start with the fact that I eventually reached an impasse and did not refinance with Better.com.

The process started fairly positively, but they requested information about my company that does not belong to me (I am an officer and part owner). By their analysis they required detailed information about the company on a loan that was replacing one in good standing at a higher interest rate. They agreed to check with their underwriters about that request.

They followed that request with a completely over-reaching request for them to contact my company and ask for any information they wanted, including all of the information I had already refused to provide. When I balked at signing a form that was so broad reaching, they claimed to not be able to move forward without the company's tax returns and a profit and loss statement.

This information is known by only a handful of people, and I wasn't about to provide it to a third party whose privacy statement allows them to share it dozens of "affiliated" companies.

I was very disappointed since the ownership of the company was not new, and my current loan was provided without the company data. It was also requested in that case, but they understood that it was unreasonable to ask for data I do not own.

I still gave them 2 stars as I believe if I had not been honest about my ownership they would not have requested more information than, in my opinion, they are entitled the process would have been much smoother.

Nonetheless, I will not do business with, nor would I ever refer, better.com.

reviewTitle: Not as quick a process as it seems, But Quality Customer Service
reviewDescription: I started the process in November, but did not close until January. Expectations were a 30 day closing/refi. Better mortgage, a new player in the online mortgage industry, does have their act together virtually, a nice website, easy to navigate, automated tracking system... all work seamlessly as you go thru various steps of completeness and providing them with items they need to get your mortgage done.  And it may not be their fault, in today's economy with trying to deal with 3rd party vendors such as credit checking, title companies and appraisers; but, they are very hard pressed to get anything done in 30 days. Including 2nd and 3rd party closing teams... Most of the things being done 'outside' of their organization were agonizingly slow.  However, their in person (and online) points of contact were always very helpful, polite, and knowledgeable and kept me up to date regularly.   I give them 4 stars because they should do and be a little bit better on estimating loan closings.. know their markets a bit better if they're going to do nationwide lending.

reviewTitle: The best mortgage experience you can get
reviewDescription:
First of all, let's address the elephant in the room. CEO made a mistake. Could he have done a better job firing people? Yes. But he has the right to fire, and this situation has been blown out of proportion! Most negative reviewers who comment on this situation never had any experience with this company. It must have been super frustrating for Better to get so many negative service reviews from haters that have nothing to do with the core service itself!!!

Now let's do a proper review of the service from the actual customer who just closed on his first house. As a first-time homebuyer, I had lots of concerns & questions about the process, and approval, as every situation is different. Better made the whole process very efficient and easy. As a millennial, I appreciate a technology-driven company that modernizes what used to be a broken process. Compared to traditional lenders Better is a century ahead! Before starting with Better, I took quotes from Chase, Rocket Morgage, and Citizen Bank, none of these companies could give me the service and convenience of Better. 

Better’s website’s dashboard is custom built from the ground up for an optimized user experience. You see everything there, the quote or estimate gets updated automatically every time they get new info. All missing documents show up as tasks and you know exactly what's needed, what's missing, what needs to be re-uploaded, etc. All numbers are explained. You even sign the documents right inside the same system, no need to go to 3rd party sites! Customer support is always available for help. Chat, phone, email. Whatever you want. Forget traditional mortgage companies where you need to wait a day for your broker to call back if he doesn't forget. Better is set up so any member in your assigned mortgage team can help you and answer any questions. In one of my first calls, when I was deciding what company to go with, the agent spent about 40 minutes with me patiently answering all my questions about the quote and the process, making me confident in Better. I was very impressed by the knowledge and the attitude. Personal gratitudes to Amelia Brown.

I liked that all the tasks are done online, no need to call or email. I called only a handful of times, as the website was self-explanatory for most of the process and I was getting enough connections and updates from the team to stay on top of what was going on. So there was no need to call! I was able to get a very good rate and the closing cost was lower than expected. My agent got a call to schedule a house appraisal within 30 minutes of me locking the rate, even before we had a signed contract for the house! Where else you can get this? No hidden fees or surprises at closing. We were able to close in under one month! Even my real estate agent, lawyer, and the guy from the title company were impressed by the process and the efficiency as they have never worked with Better before.

I can write a lot more but I want to end with a big thank you to my broker Cristina Alvarez and the team for your hard work! I'm sure that lots of things are happening behind that awesome dashboard to make the process smooth and easy for the customer! You are the best!

reviewTitle: I’m rating this a two for the following…
reviewDescription:
I’m rating this a two for the following reasons and none of them had to do with our agent Brian Moran, he was a 5 star for sure!!! 

When we started the process everything was going smoothly at the initial call advising we were pre approved, we started out with a contact Oscar, he was less than efficient. 

After the shake up with Better, we no longer had Oscar, we now had Jose Escobar. While Jose did his best, it was the weeks of Christmas and New Years so no one was working, lots of vacations, including Jose. 

There were things that Jose should have done prior to closing, that did not happen. The morning of closing 12/30, I received an automated reply stating that Better gave employees time off to recharge and he would be back after the 1st if the New Year. A very scary email to get when you’re needing someone to help with closing. During the closing the title company could not reach anyone at Better. We gad a 10:00 am closing, the funds were not funded. Panic is putting it lightly. To find out that Jose did not send final tasks to be approved for the wire. 

We got connected to AB Schrimscher, she was AMAZING!!!! She took care of everything immediately. The funds finally wired at 2;00 pm, we stayed at the title company until funds were transferred to get our key to our new home. 

The concept of Better is great, your one stop shop. However, the staffing seems to need a little work. 

Again, Brian Moran and AB Schrimscher were definitely both top notch, 5 star employees.

However, with my new purchase from a reputable builder, I stepped out of the box and decided to shop my mortgage options. Better Mortgage blew away the orher offers I recieved from builder mortgage company as well as quicken loan with the lowest rate, and cash to close estimates. There were some hiccups along the way but using same tittle company as builder recommended, and having a didcated loan office Anika who was there with me throughout the process helping to make any adjustments corrections that were needed prior to closing. This is the main reason why i am rating a 5. If there was something that i suggest they improve is the timeline underwriting took accessing uploaded appraisals by third party. This minor hiccup caused my closing to be delayed 1 day. Overall though i must say the processes, portal experience was pretty smooth and once i uploaded all docs at the beginning of the laon process, there were minimum request during closing. One last thing is that on top of the no points deal that i recieved, i was able to recieve a 2k Amex card statement credit which i should see in 1 ro 2 months.

reviewDescription:
Pros: 
 + Better.com has competitive rates
 + Somebody picks up the phone when you call
 + They were patient and answered all my questions
 + Online platform has a notion of Tasks that are usually easy to follow
 + Closing went relatively smoothly
Cons:
- I was excited about online platform and automation to get through the process faster, but there are a LOT of bugs in the system and often I could not complete tasks online and had to make a call anyway.
- I end up uploading the same documents multiple times and going back and forth with different people on the phone
- It can be hard to get a scheduled appointment with home advisor
- It took way more interactions than I expected to complete the process.
- Closing costs are pretty high.
- Music on hold is very depressing

reviewDescription:
Let me preface my comments by stating that these are our experiences, yours may differ.

Better.com was absolutely the best experience I have ever had with a mortgage (ours was a refinance).  It started out with a terrific rate, and then Mark became our loan advisor and things just kept getting better.  He explained what we needed to supply, why it was needed, and kept us appraised of everything that was taking place from the start of the approval process all the way through to the closing of our loan.  He, and Better, delivered exactly what they said they would, and did it in the time frame they said it would happen.  We didn't have to make multiple trips to a bank, we were able to do most of the paperwork online.  The title company sent a person to our home to finish the process (who does that).  Our loan was funded in less than 48 hours.  Again, I can't state it enough, Better.com and Mark and our closing agent far exceeded our wildest expectations.  I would absolutely recommend them to friends, family, and anyone else needing a mortgage loan or refinance.  Thanks to all of their staff for a really well done job.

reviewTitle: Worst experience with a lender ever!
reviewDescription:
Worst experience with a lender ever!! 

They will mislead you that all is well until it’s time to close. Then the fun begins. It’s almost as if they don’t actually look at you loan until it’s time to close and then the flags start coming… 

Firstly they don’t understand property tax in CA and more specifically what prop 13 means for it. Because of that they screwed up our loan and required a larger deposit by 100k! We agree as our EMD is on the line, we sign docs a day late… they then refuse to fund unless more arbitrary docs are signed. They are almost impossible to get a hold of, don’t help when you do and pass you around whenever they can. 0 customer service skills. They end up funding three days late after I’ve been on a call with s manager for hours. They also fired our loan officer during the closing which really didn’t help… 

Long story short, stay far far far away from them, if they don’t completely screw you over they’ll delay you significantly and cause numerous other problems. 

It’s simply just a terrible company.

reviewTitle: Better is convenient and workers are responsive, but robotic - no customized approach.
reviewDescription:
Better is convenient as you can do everything online, the team is responsive and the rates are attractive. However, it has a downside: there isn't much room for customization of approach, consideration of different circumstances, etc. It's very robotic: "that's the rule, that's how we do it". 
I also had some surprises on values during the process, which were corrected in the closing day, but only then. The taxes were over provisioned and my monthly payment was looking $500 more than what it actually was at the initial estimate, which made me uneasy. The closing costs also fluctuated a lot... I didn't have this same experience with other companies, it was stressful. 
All in all, everything went well, but it wasn't super smooth. 
One very good thing though: they were extremely helpful to get my verified pre-approval letter out the door. Worked very closely with me to expedite it, which I highly appreciated. 
Again, the team is responsive and polite, but limited, which makes their approach very robotic, not customized.

reviewTitle: Did not file my refinancing paperwork properly and now my title is messed up.
reviewDescription: I refinanced with Better.com in October 2020. The refinance process was pretty smooth. They were professional and responsive. If you refinance with Better, be wary that they will not file all the paperwork and that it will come back to bite you in the butt at a later date. What a headache. I have contacted them twice in the past week, and received no response.

reviewDescription: I think this is a good concept if you have no issues and don't need to interact with a qualified person.  It fits into our digital society and how we transact today.  In my case, I didn't close on a refinance I was seeking because Better.com changed the lender credits (money to offset closing costs) after the rate was locked (a $2,500 reduction in lender credits).  They claimed the change was due to a lower property value based on the appraisal.  They sent their contracted appraiser who came in with a value 10% lower than Redfin and 6% lower than Zillow.  The whole process felt like "bait-and switch" using a contrived property value to justify taking more profit for them.  Once this happened, trying to get a straight answer from someone was like trying to find a human to cancel an online subscription for something.   In the future, for something as complex as a real estate financing transaction, I think dealing with humans face to face is still the best route.  This isn't ready for prime time in my opinion.  Maybe some day.

They first said, don't worry, you're portal is not showing this offer because they just show the initial offer, just keep clicking and you'll be fine. Then, after choosing to pay for the appraisal up front, the portal says again, click this offer to lock it in which -- but it still wasn't what they offered us via email!! At that point, they said, oh, the market shifted, sorry. Ok, if the market is "shifting" that fast, we'll go with a different company. They didn't care. A day later, they said it was due to our credit being pulled (although they already had done that). Bottom line, they offered us a fraudulent offer to get us to "lock-in" and when we did, they couldn't make up their mind with their "why" the offer changed. Don't trust them!

reviewTitle: This was honestly one of the worst…
reviewDescription:
This was honestly one of the worst experiences I have had. 
My representative did not explain things clearly along the way and just as i would have one letter finally sent from my bank (which was also like pulling teeth) another would expire and I would have to start again.
I had a charged off HELOC to deal with and when the bank that held the HELOC could not produce a letter with the required verbiage they requested my representative call them. He did so under protest and then was rude to them to the point that i had to apologize for his behavior when I next spoke to them.
Then to top it off just when it was all finally done (after TWO MONTHS) the numbers WERE NOT what we had discussed. My representative had completely disregarded the requests I had made weeks prior. It was cause for a panic attack.
This is the largest obligation in my life and to have the numbers be different in the last possible moment caused me to lose much faith in Better as a lender. 
In the end I told my representative to stop texting me and called into the 800 number and asked to speak to a manger. No one likes to be a Karen. :/ 
At this point, after strongly complaining, did I feel cared for enough to move forward with the loan ( but mostly it was because I didnt want to start somewhere new).
I would suggest better and more comprehensive training to your staff and perhaps with older customers like myself more phone calls would help over incessant texting which only compounded confusions.
In the end Better made it better but it should not have been bad to begin with.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hey Heather, I'm so sorry to hear about the frustration during your experience. Please reach out to us at support@better.com if you'd like to discuss further.

reviewTitle: They had a great rate, but then...
reviewDescription:
They had a great rate, then flags started popping up everywhere. We're super internet savvy, do nearly everything electronically, and have refinanced dozens of homes, so when we found Better on Nerd Wallet (shame on you Nerds - we used to trust you!), we thought Better was made for us. We were wrong. I'll try to list the issues we encountered clearly, from big to little:

1. Rate lock for 30 days, but then they become the bottleneck, and before you know it many thousands in extension fees are requested of you.
2. Look out for their disclosed network of companies. Part of their model is to immediately throw your business to a sister company to create fee income for themselves, often at inappropriate times/sequence. After looking, I realized their sister company fees for most things are higher than you can get elsewhere.
3. Very junior mortgage professionals. Our main person was decent, but then went on vacation and we got a total newb, who had no answers and escalated to his "team" which did absolutely nothing, repeatedly.
4. When leaving questions for "the team" online about certain docs, status, or process, I don't think we received a single definitive timely response or explanation. They clearly use an offshore back office team, and it doesn't work. This contributes to the Better Bottleneck.
5. When submitting one set of documents, this would spawn an entire new set of document requests, even ones that weren't applicable. This went on exponentially for the first two weeks. This, and the need to review (everything is "in review"), and answer questions also contributes to the Better Bottleneck.
6. During the process we finally had to arrange for closing. Then I found out that they don't do electronic closings. Our state has done pure electronic closings since 2008...but Better doesn't do them at all. 
7. The sequencing of actions, fees and referrals, lack of web-based capabilities (incl. the use of an old fashioned physical appraisal), poor service, and Better's Bottleneck, struck me as being manipulative and deliberate. Kind of like one might find in a New York or Jersey boiler room operation if it were being run by somebody that didn't know mortgage lending very well...and had even less familiarity with West coast mortgage lending. So I checked their location...guess what.

I know you're "sorry for my experience" folks, and that you'll "send my information to somebody who cares", but you won't change a thing. If this company goes public I would stay far away from it. I smell law suits coming.

I was very pleased with Better throughout the homebuying process from how easy the pre-approval process was through to the closing. The website is very easy to use and the task feature helps to keep things organized. I also give five stars to Kiana Brown, my advisor, as she was always extremely professional, knowledgeable and helpful. I will definitely recommend Better to others.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thank you for leaving us a review!

Unfortunately, while you DO get an uncompetitive rate, the process of getting a mortgage is still just as maddening and stupid as it ever was:
Explaining the same thing to an underwriter 3 times.  People quitting or getting reassigned in the middle of processing.
People going on vacation the day before closing, leaving a contact in their place that has also gone on vacation.

It's baffling and frustrating to have a 20% downpayment, excellent credit, and stand to make a lender 50% on the lifetime of a million dollar mortgage, and explain every nook and cranny of your finances ten times.  

I came expecting white glove service in exchange for the price, but I might as well have just gone to Bank of America.  Not a compelling service, and not recommended.

reviewTitle: Be prepared for frustrating inaccuracies, delays, and more delays…
reviewDescription: This was my first experience with Better and I am sadly disappointed.  I selected them for their great rates, online process, and the philosophy of no more frustrating mortgage experiences.  Better’s online process is generally pretty simple and steam-lined, but the process behind the scenes is a mess.  I had received pre-approval and locked in my rate in early August, expecting to close in mid-October.  ~ 7 weeks into the process, in late September, I am notified that they can not offer the loan to purchase the properties as outlined in my purchase agreement.  No one had read and understood the purchase agreement closely, even after I called attention to some uniquities in our agreement.  So we work through that by amendments to the purchase agreement, and it’s just been one thing after another since that.  6 revised purchase agreements later, 4 appraisal revisions, revising the loan structure twice, surprise decreases in promised lender credits, uncertainty on status of title search.  I am beginning to think they really wanted me to cancel…maybe they aren’t making enough off of my $500K loan.  I was scheduled to close on this loan tomorrow (third closing date because the first two couldn’t be met due to delays).  As of 6PM, I still have not obtained a copy of the final closing disclosure outlining settlement costs.  I’ve been asking for it all week since I’ve already experienced closing get rescheduled twice.  I was told by my loan agent that final balancing was underway (for 2 days), and then never updated again.  Needless to say, I am not going to closing at 9 am tomorrow because I am walking away from this lender.  The service was terrible and no one with authority was accountable to me as the customer throughout this process.  I got a bunch of apologies.  I did appreciate the positive attitudes of the people I dealt with though-out the process, they were very polite and apologetic, but this organization is poorly managed.  I am probably the profile for the ideal borrower - stable employment, high income, well documented cash and assets, near perfect credit report, etc. Their process is over-reliant on third-party affiliates whom they have no control over.  I would say that I pretty much drove the processing of this loan through the entire process. Was really willing to give this new company a try.  Started out great, but in the end I’ve spent too much of my own time to get through the process.  No call back on status at 12 hours before settlement is an absolute disgrace for customer service.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hey Rebecca, I'm so sorry to hear about the frustration during your experience. Please reach out to us at support@better.com if you'd like to discuss further.

reviewDescription: Your "tasks" system has some problems. From beginning to end. For example I got the last 3 tasks that I was suppose to get 24 hrs before closing 4 days after closing ,so knowing how your computer is I did those tasks, like confirmed the notary info 4 days after meeting with the notary. Another example I showed my bank statement showing I had plenty of cash in the bank  but had to later show I could come up with the $300. needed for closing. Yup ,I could go on and on.  Quite a few tasks the computer had me do 2 or three times. I kidded with my rep. that the one email request keep coming to me 4 times as to if I wanted to bring the $300. to closing or add it into the loan. Other things I brought up repeatedly weren't  responded to until your computer program apparently  thought it was time. Like you kept using a 2 month old payoff number. I never spoke to a human once until of course the notary at closing. It made me feel like the company is run by AI's. I have owned and flipped a lot on properties as a hobby in my life so I have done 10 or 20 mortgages in my life. What I come away with is these young generation Z'ers who don't want human interaction (like my teenage kids) might like never talking to a human. But for me things like have to sign the HOA form you call Planned Unit Development when I furnished a statement from the HOA that it is a voluntary club not a legal HOA was just silly. Now that it's over I am somewhat satisfied but was very frustrated during the process. I also thought paying extra to extend the rate lock was unfair because it was Better dragging their feet ,I was ready to close day one.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hey Don, I'm so sorry to hear about the frustration during your experience. Please reach out to us at support@better.com if you'd like to discuss further.

reviewTitle: Will lie to you about getting a rebate! Incompetent and frustrating too boot.
reviewDescription:
Better.com promised a 1% rebate on the purchase price of my home if I worked with their referred realtor. I spoke to multiple people, multiple times that all promised this in writing and verbally. Guess what, more than a month after closing I still haven't received the rebate and no one will return my calls or emails. The last I heard was weeks ago and they were working on it. Not true! It's not worth the headache. I've spent hours trying to get them to deal with this and I can't get through to anyone. They are utterly incompetent but claim that they will always be available. A local loan officer will actually return your calls and keep their promises because they have to be accountable to everyone else in their local community, not like this giant corporation. 

Also, Alan Youngberg was my person and lied through his teeth throughout the whole process. Would never return my calls, emails, or texts. Wish I had walked away before it was too late.

The other people who made these promises included Lawrence Lee with Better Realty and Timothy Abogado/TJ Abogado.

The process of dealing with all of their "tasks" is a nightmare. It's all a poorly designed website made by someone who knows nothing about UI and seems to take pleasure in making things as frustrating as possible. Because it's a big corporation that is trying to streamline everything, it ends up taking way longer than necessary. You have no option to just send your documents to them or answer the questions directly. If the question doesn't apply to you, good luck getting them to take the "task" off your to do list and let you move to the next step.

On top of that, the majority of the people I spoke to literally understood less about mortgages than I, a layperson, did. Including someone (who I am feeling kind enough at this point to not name) who was supposed to answer my questions about something on my initial closing disclosure who kept insisting we should look at the loan estimate instead because it was easier to understand. When I tried to explain that made no sense because it was no longer up to date, my question had to do with the closing disclosure numbers, not the loan estimate numbers, and that I felt confident looking at the closing disclosure, he finally disclosed that HE did not know how to interpret it.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hi Ellie, I'm so sorry to hear about this - please reach out to us at support@better.com if you'd like to discuss further.

reviewTitle: Second refi in a matter of months…
reviewDescription:
This was my second refinance over the past couple of months. Refinancing my investment property didn’t go as smoothly as my residence. I first applied in July and had an appraisal done. The appraiser (who was “new and unfamiliar” to the area) assessed my house at $151 a foot. I provided details as to why that was incorrect and my request to reevaluate the appraisal we denied. I asked for another appraisal to be done and was told the only way to have another appraisal was to go through the application process again. 

At no point was I told I would be subject to “worst case” pricing or additional points if I go through another application. So I applied again hoping someone more reasonable would appraise my house. Someone with more knowledge or the area. I applied at the end of august and zero communication for weeks. I had to follow up with your team to find out when I was going to have the appraisal scheduled. I was told the appraisal company wasn’t reaching out Bc they saw I had a recent appraisal done but they were able to get the ball rolling again. So a month after I paid for the appraisal it was completed and viola!! They appraised it at $260,000, $24,000 more than the previous appraiser. 

So I’m disappointed in the communication or lack there of and I’m highly unsatisfied that I was penalized the way I was especially being a two time borrower who has nearly $600,000 in home loans with you all now. If the investment refi was my first experience I wouldnt have refinanced my primary residence. This is an offstage complaint. I am not taking this onstage because I would hope someone with authority will respond to my complaint.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thank you for the honest review - we are sending your feedback to our team.

reviewDescription: I will say that this is my 2nd refinance with Better.  Unlike the first refi, this experience was stressful, unorganized, irritating, showed lack of attention to customer service, lack of communication skills and unprofessional as if I was dealing with people who did not know how to do their job.  Too many hands on the file and no communication amongst coworkers.  Repetitive and redundancy.  Closing was a nightmare had to sign 2x wrong information when it was explained to make sure there wouldn't be any mistake.  It was as if the questions asked and the answers given were outright ignored, thus when Notary arrived it was ALL wrong, but was told don't worry still sign call Better let them know. It will be ok.  So then had to resign a second time and only to find out a week later, only because I emailed Ashley asking when is funding taking place since it was to be 3 days after signing and then was told oh, you have tasks to do.  No auto emails were sent out letting me know when things were needed on a consistent basis.  Emails were unanswered for 2 to 3 days, until I complained and included manager did it get better but only to revert back to the same non-communication at closing.  I signed on September 27 and October 6.   Didn't get any calls emails or texts letting me know they needed the rescission docs yet again until I emailed Ashly on the 13th, because I'm getting notified and calls from Mr. Cooper my existing loan that I'm being charged a late fee and need to make payment by the 14th or incur  another 98.00 in  fees as well as Fremont Bank notifying me my payment is due.  She then sends me an email saying oh, we need you to sign the recission letter.  We can't fund unless we have that.  Well we all signed the recission letter 2x before and now 3 is a charm.  I told her my displeasure how I felt and she said Sorry, but at least we are at the end that's all we need and you will be funded on the 12th.  Well it was the 13th.  Shows how much attention to detail she had with my file.  So after that is done.  I get an email 10-14 a.m. stating FANNIE MAE guidelines needs and updated letter from your CPA to close we can't close unless the letter from your CPA is dated 10 days of the closing.  Well, had there not been all these mistakes or had someone known we had to sign yet a 2nd time they should have taken care of it then.  I am on vacation for my birthday and I have to deal with this, recontact my CPA to update the letter send it back as they tell me that this is the last thing they need to fund.  Well yet again another display of incompetency and such disorganization.  I get it done and receive an email hours later telling me congratulations your loan is funded.  Of course I go check and see and it is NOT.  I was told by Reilly Carter to go ahead and pay my mortgage to ensure that I don't get a negative  reporting on my Credit Report as that was my main concern.  I could go on and on with other issues throughout this process, but I would never refi with Better again.  This was so stressful, and I just felt like another number.  No personal touch did not feel like I was a value to your company but just another computer-generated file.  The left hand is definitely not letting the right hand know what they are doing as if everyone is running around with their heads cut off.  I would not refer Better to any of my colleagues or friends....   I really hope you all can get organized and perhaps train your staff how to communicate and fix the algorithms on your system to better streamline the process and notify clients when tasks are needed or you need additional information.  And definitely DON'T have clients sign closing docs when you have an incomplete file.  Signing the closing docs to a consumer means we are DONE.  I should NOT have to be running around signing rescission letters 3x having my CPA write and rewrite letters after the fact.   One dissatisfied customer's experience spreads faster than wildfire than 12 happy customers.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hi Andrea, I'm so sorry fo the frustration - please reach out to us at support@better.com if you'd like to discuss further.

reviewDescription: We had an absolutely wonderful experience with Better.com in our recent mortgage loan refinance in three very important and critical ways: (1) we locked in an historically low 2.5 percent interest rate on a 30-year mortgage loan with zero points; (2) the entire process, from initial loan application to closing took only about 18 calendar days; and (3) there were zero loan fees -- repeat, ZERO -- along with title costs that were incredibly low.  We're still wondering how Better.com made any money.  By contrast, last year we did a refinance with a regional loan company in which we paid over twice as much in title costs plus nearly $1200 in loan origination fees -- what a ripoff  compared to Better.com.  As for their loan processing and customer service, we also felt that they were exemplary: all of our borrower "tasks" were always clearly explained by their web-based system and then immediately processed.  Similarly, any questions we had (and we had quite a few) were quickly and courteously answered by email or phone calls.  Our experience is proof that Better.com is a shining example of Fintech at its finest.  Better.com really is better!!!

reviewTitle: Empty promises and excuses. Do not use them!!!!
reviewDescription:
The initial process was quick and I got approval fairly quick as well. But afterwards, everything else fell apart.
We had to postpone the first closing due to better.com scheduling the closing and then not providing any explanation around the cost on the disclosure until 1 hour before the closing at which time I canceled the closing.

The second attempt for whatever reason better.com canceled without explanation and with some excuses about the title company having messed up and they have "no control" over them.

On the third attempt, they promised me to have everything in place and confirmed 24 hours before the scheduled closing. It's now 7 hours before the scheduled closing and I have still not heard from them nor received any updated paperwork, scheduling information, etc. 

DO NOT USE BETTER.COM

The only thing you will get from them is empty promises and excuses if you hear from them at all.

reviewTitle: Please save yourself from this experience
reviewDescription:
I'm not normally one to write long reviews, but if I can save just one person from having an experience like mine, I have to in this case.

Better.com is the worst kind of digital company. They want all of the cost savings associated with being an online only originator, but don't deliver at all on the experience. Be prepared for ridiculous delays (we will get back to you within 24 hours claim is a joke), being forced to submit the same documentation over and over again with no explanation, and in the end having random decisions with no reasonable explanation.

I applied with high credit score and 15 year employment history with the same company. The mortgage amount was less than my annual income and I have more than enough cash in the bank to pay it off. For this low risk case, they somehow managed to drag out the process for four months. The low point for me was having to submit the same form for the fifth time after having it repeatedly rejected with no explanation (it was finally revealed that I had to check a box with a pen rather than typing an x on the computer). After uncountable similarly frustrating experiences, I thought I had finally emerged from their torturous process when I got my approval notification and a task to schedule my closing. How wrong I was! They follow this up with a text saying that they will now not move forward with my loan. Their stated reason: I travel too much for work. I don't even know how to respond to that.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hey Derrick, I'm so sorry to hear about the frustration - please reach out to us at support@better.com if you'd like to discuss further.

reviewDescription: Their flash tech savvy appearance is merely a charade. reviewTitle: My experience with Better.com was a huge waste of time, money, and emotional duress
reviewDescription:
Update: I was able to get a formal letter of loan denial from better after writing this review. Hilariously, the reasons listed for my denial are not any of the ones I listed below. They seem to be picked at random (Too many delinquent accounts? I have no delinquent accounts) so I guess the bots at Better just randomly selected me to get turned down. Sweet.

--

This is a sham business with Kafka-esque communication and customer service process. Do NOT be taken in by their low interest rates and tech-savvy appearance. I was preapproved for a mortgage by Better on 7/19/21. At multiple times in this process, I asked my contact, Julia, if I would be approved considering I have an unconventional income and have been denied loans in the past due to this. She assured me it would not be a problem. (In retrospect I believe Julia had no professional knowledge and never really knew what she was talking about, nor did any of the dozens of minions I was rerouted to whenever I tried to contact my loan consultant.) With that information I booked travel to my desired location. I was given ANOTHER pre-approval letter on 8/19/21 to reflect the house's cost. I put in an offer on 8/19/21 and it was accepted 8/20/21.

On 9/20/21, one month later, after putting down earnest money, paying for inspections and insurance, booking another trip for closing, expediting my divorce (really) to confirm I had no financial liability in my separation, and paying a $550 "rate locking fee" I was told I had been denied. The reasons I was given could have been detected in the pre-approval process, which is why I asked over and over again at the top of this process. Worse, they determined that the apartment I rent negatively affected my debt-to-income ratio. I DISCLOSED MY RENT IN THE PRE-APPROVAL PROCESS. My loan consultant Jonathan denied this. Later, he took it back, confirming that I had indeed disclosed my rent. (LOL.) They assumed I wouldn't be paying it anymore. Why would you assume that? It was as if nobody had looked at my financials before then.

Worse, I was instantly locked out of my account and can't contact anyone there or get any documentation of my application and denial. My emails and calls go unanswered (my calls are often just dropped.) I have no recourse but to leave reviews like this and hope nobody else gives this rickety tin can of a company their business. (I also suspect many of these positive reviews, which sound suspiciously familiar, are plants.)
reviewCompanyResponse: Hi Emily, I'm so sorry to hear about the frustration - please reach out to us at support@better.com if you'd like to discuss further.

reviewTitle: It started out great and then it all…
reviewDescription: It started out great and then it all went downhill. Multiple requests for the same documentation, lack of understanding of anything that didn't follow their template/check list. Not sure if they have actual loan officers with field experience nor an underwriting team that knows the business. I'm almost 2 months in and still not sure when I will close. Their appraisal company came in 20k less than previous appraisal with no explanation and used lower comps. And there are hidden fees so beware! I should have continued with Rocket Mortgage because this has been a nightmare and I was a real loan officer for 5 years.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hi Monika, I'm so sorry to hear about the frustration - please reach out to us at support@better.com if you'd like to discuss further.

reviewDescription: Started everything was good until a small problem happened with my credit that was being remedied but they stop contacting me and taking my calls then without any other contact or documentation in any way told me they wouldn't go the loan....poor customer service and horrible service...

Started process with them on May 4th, supposedly cleared title 2 weeks later.  Claim was that they were "looking for an appraiser" since my property had some acreage attached and "only certain people were able to appraise these types".  Got several follow-up emails asking about the parcels and their descriptions, literally asking the exact same questions.  They couldn't wrap their brains around the fact that the mortgage covered more than one parcel.  At some point during this, I got an email saying they had to look for another appraiser because "I never responded to messages from the appraiser." This was a complete lie, confirmed.  After 2 months of waiting, I finally called a supervisor, who told me in fact that the appraisal wasn't ordered in May, but at the END OF JULY because of their incompetence dealing with the two parcels.  So after 4 months of waiting, lots of back and forth, and lots of lies, I get an email saying they are closing my application because they "can't find anyone willing to appraise the property" when I know for a fact the company who appraised it less than 2 years ago is still operating and not very far from the property (about an hour away), not uncommon for rural properties.  AVOID THESE LIARS LIKE THE PLAGUE.  And to think the email said, please consider us in the future.  I've never had a team of more incompetent amateurs all the way up to the supervisors in my life...  yeah, right.
reviewCompanyResponse: I'm so sorry to hear about the frustration so far, but thank you for letting me know. Please reach out to our Client Success Team at support@better.com to discuss further.

reviewTitle: 2 Stars for rates and speed - The rest was a mess...Updated to 1 Star for Servicing Transfers
reviewDescription:
I used to work in the mortgage industry so I have a pretty good idea of how this process works. They get 2 stars for having the lowest rates and for fast processing and underwriting times. The rest of the experience was a bit of a mess and clunky. So you start with putting in what type of loan you're trying to qualify for then it asks you for the normal documents. I was doing a refinance and rolling in the closing costs so I wouldn't need any cash to close. It didn't correctly add in the amounts at the start of the loan and then also asked me for bank statements to show sufficient funds to close. Once again, rolling in costs so don't need to provide that. Had to reach out to processor to adjust both issues. Went through processing and UW very quickly as I had sent them everything they needed quickly. Moved onto closing, what a mess. First closing disclosure (CD) was correct so we set up signing. Then as signing approached CD was changed and all of a sudden I needed to bring around $1,000 cash to close. Emailed and messaged closer to correct. No correspondence until after my signing. I was left messaging my notary during the day saying we might not actually be signing because the docs were wrong. So had to cancel 1st signing. For the next week we played try to correct the CD. I found out, yes in fact they made a mistake on the CD. What followed was around 8 different CDs. Good luck trying to keep up with all of those and the changes and which one is most recent, because their system is poorly set up to track that. It took a week to finally get it corrected and set up new signing. When the notary came, there were 2 different CDs again including an incorrect one. Finally got everything taken care of and the loan funded. They transferred it to The Money Source (TMS) to service. Had an awful customer experience with them, where their VP of Customer Experience had to get involved and told me that their customer care team had sent me the wrong information.  Now my loan servicing is being transferred on the day my 1st payment is due on a holiday weekend, so we'll see how that goes. Hopefully as it moves further away from Better.com my experience will get better.

Update on the servicing being transferred: Why in the world would Better.com think it is a good idea to transfer servicing on the day the 1st payment is due!?!? I'm 11 days into the month now and have not been able to make my payment, I've tried online and over the phone and it will not work. Who thought it would be a good idea to have this be the timing of the service transfer? I can not stress enough how much I do not recommend working with Better.com. I've had mortgages with Wells Fargo and US Bank in the past and never had this much trouble. I am now subtracting the 1 extra star I gave for rates and speed due to the servicing fiasco.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hey Ben, I am very sorry to hear about your experience and totally understand the frustration. Thank you for making me aware of this. I'm sending your feedback to our team so we can make some improvements for the future. If you'd like to discuss further, please feel free to reach out to us at support@better.com.

reviewDescription: Five stars (rounding from about 93%). First, here's what I found smooth and solid: everything from entry to the transition from loan preparation to closing. Your website is very user-friendly in design, layout, navigation, content, feedback to user, and access to support. I really loved the educational materials. Also, the processes I encountered on my account page on the website were tightly linked to personnel who were highly accessible, and provided timely, thorough, and clear explanations and guidance. That made my experience of the loan application, analysis, approval, and preparation processes really seamless and low-stress. I'm a newbie at finance! However, I felt like we hit a wall when we got to the loan closure process, insofar as I felt like I was working blind and alone. I didn't know what to expect. So there was a lapse in support there, that I initially attributed as a personnel issue. However it may also (or entirely) have been structural; the information guiding me through the loan process during the closing steps were nowhere near as granular as they had been during other steps (e.g., official emails from BMC tying to notices on my account page; or even the time-line on my account page). Now that we're in the funding phase, I feel like I'm back in business with the BMC whose praises I've been singing to friends and family again. I'm getting an email from someone every day or so, explaining what's happening, and what to expect in the path ahead. What a relief! I hope these remarks have been clear. I don't actually know the names of the various phases of the loan process, so I've tried to guess which terms would make these smooth (mostly) and bumpy (closing) waypoints clear. Thanks for what has been, overall, a great experience. Cheers, Robin

This has been one of the easiest home closings I have ever had. Better has streamlined the entire process. I loved that they offer many rate options up front and depict the advantages of each. Their website rocks! The last loan company I went with was amateur hour changing the 
cost every few days. With better, i knew exactly what everything cost. Great job guys!
reviewCompanyResponse: Thank you Tom!

reviewTitle: This was the best experience that I…
reviewDescription:
This was the best experience that I have ever had.  The refinancing process was so easy.  Everything was tasked out so, I knew exactly was needed from me.  
Closing was easy, and I signed my documents at my job. I loved this experience of refinancing, and I was able to pick my own interest rate. My next time buying or refinancing I will use Better.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thanks Harris!

reviewDescription: Better.com provided an exceptional experience for my home loan refinance. They walked me through every step of the way, were completely transparent, and I never felt like they were trying to push something on me that wasn't to my benefit. Every employee I interacted with was extremely professional and pleasant to talk with. Thanks for everything! I will be recommending to friends and family!
reviewCompanyResponse: Thanks Ashton!

reviewDescription: The Better refinance was so smooth. I liked how I could look at all the different loan estimates prior to committing to refinancing. This helped me see the different rates and how they affect closing costs.  I was able to pick the loan that would work out best for me rather than relying on a person to run the numbers for me. The explanations videos about credits and points were extremely helpful and easy to follow. After committing to refinancing, I couldn’t have asked for an easier process. The tasks to complete were easy to follow and I could upload documents on my own time. This was very helpful because my job limits me from being able to answer phone calls. The online component was perfect.  The Better team was very responsive and helpful with any concerns I had. All in all, the refinance wrapped up in less than 4 weeks. I would recommend Better to my closest friends.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thank you Boots!

reviewDescription: I was skeptical at first about 100% online refinancing but it turned out to be the best decision I ever made. They made the process so easy! Very knowledgeable staff and eager to help. Would definitely recommend Better Mortgage.

reviewDescription: I put in my application on May 3. We closed on May 20. It seems like they have people working 24 hours a day. They were prompt and always available to answer questions.

reviewTitle: Refinancing a home is always a pain
reviewDescription: Refinancing a home is always a pain. The Better online tools were almost painless. Couple of little hick-ups along the way, but everything went well.

reviewDescription: I found the process of refinancing a loan was very easy and the staff at Better were readily available to answer any questions.  Even more important were  the costs to refinance; Better’s fees were much lower than the mortgage companies I have used in the past.

reviewDescription: Simple and easy, never left my house and refi done in less than a month! Unheard of!  Great response when needed and fast.

reviewTitle: I found your online process convenient…
reviewDescription:
I found your online process convenient and efficient. I was very impressed.

The only bump in the road was on choice of closing attorney. In SC an attorney must close the loan.  I chose an attorney on the appropriate task/form, but he was never contacted.  As we moved closer and closer to the closing, I asked that he be notified and involved. Three days before closing, when it was apparent that Better had moved the process along without him, he graciously bowed out of the transaction.  The attorney that Better sent to close the loan was very, very good. However, he wasn't my choice. Please review how this was handled and be open to this part of the process in the future.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thanks Jim!

reviewDescription: I am in the mortgage business, and I was very impressed with your system and also the amount of communication I received from the loan officer, and especially the processor and closer. Exceeded my expectations. Thank you.

reviewDescription: Simple, easy to understand experience. Heard from friends who used and Better made the mortgage process simpler to navigate.

Karen and Ken Schleifer
