reviewDescription: The refi through Better was super convenient and easy. I loved the Web interface and I have a hard time imagining a smoother process. Thanks Better!
reviewCompanyResponse: Thanks Mark!

reviewDescription: Better.com was able to get me a great 30 year fixed rate with no money down on a refi.  I'm saving money and very happy!
reviewCompanyResponse: Thank you for the review, Robert!

reviewTitle: This was such a cluster*uck from the…
reviewDescription:
This was such a cluster*uck from the start.  We went through the rates and I felt the taxes and insurance were not correct but they assured me they were.  When it came time for the final closing document, the monthly payment jumped up 200$ which I was not okay with.  they talked me through some other rates and I agreed to move forward with a different term to meet my needs.  We agreed on everything and they they sent me the final closing disclosure and sent someone to sign with us.  A day later they said it was wrong and we had to resign, so we set up another appointment for an morning to sign.  When we got the paperwork the number was wrong, we signed and I emailed Better and told them I thought the numbers looked off and she said I was right and we would need to SIGN a 3rd time!!!  

Slow down people!  I know you are spitting out these refinances like hot cakes but read your work.  This is your job!
reviewCompanyResponse: Hey Amy, I am very sorry to hear about your experience and totally understand the frustration. Thank you for making me aware of this. I'm sending your feedback to our team so we can make some improvements for the future. If you'd like to discuss further, please feel free to reach out to us at support@better.com.

reviewDescription: The folks we worked with were outstanding - quick to respond to questions and provide information! The mobile closing in our home was awesome. There were some duplicate requests for info which was annoying, but smooth process overall.  Definitely recommended!
reviewCompanyResponse: Thanks Shawn!

reviewTitle: Everything was going just fine until…
reviewDescription: Everything was going just fine until the application went to underwriting.  This is when everything fell apart.  The underwriters were not satisfied with my method of income.  I am a travel healthcare professional and because I work in a different state than my home residence, that was a red flag for them.  They also can't comprehend the way the travel healthcare field works in that our assignments are usually 13 weeks then we either extend or move onto a new contract.  This is also unsatisfactory to them..  This wouldn't be that big of a deal except I am 3 weeks out from time I'm supposed to close my loan for the new home.  I've paid $10,000 in an earnest deposit and money for appraisal and inspection which I will have to forfeit because of Better Mortgage.  I wish they would have told me in the very beginning before I paid thousands of dollars that my employment type was unsatisfactory to them.  Very shameful.

reviewTitle: I was a little skeptical because I had…
reviewDescription: I was a little skeptical because I had a bad experience with another Company (LD) which I fired after six Months of waiting.  I decided to try Better Homes they surpassed my on time closing expectations the first time. So I came back again for a second refinancing and no surprise they got it on time again. For clear instructions and people that actually care try Better Homes.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thanks for the review, VI!

reviewDescription: So far this has been way easier then I had imagined. I literally had no stress and I didn't feel like I was being hustled by a used car salesman! ;-)  I got a very fair deal with my refinance that will save me a good bit and I appreciate all of the help and communication along the way.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thank you, Julianna!

reviewDescription:
These guys were so patient and helpful. Made everything so streamlined and easy. 100% recommend. The whole process took around 30 days, (it could've been faster, but we made some mistakes in the process and they were super helpful offering advise within their capacity) and now we're home owners.
Thanks to Valeria, Federico, Olan and Maria.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thanks Sergio.

reviewDescription: I can't believe how quick and easy they made the entire process. By far the most streamlined loan process I've ever experienced! From the online dashboard showing all open tasks at any moment, to the closing attorney coming to my house to finish everything up, it worked well and took very little of my time or attention to complete.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thank you Mike. Let us know if there's anything else we can help with.

reviewTitle: Great rates, helpful people, would use them again
reviewDescription: Using Better.com to refinance my house was pretty darn easy.  The online forms made things go very smoothly.  When I had questions, my better.com contacts were quick and helpful.   The only complaints I had were what seemed like redundant requests for sensitive information.  I uploaded my tax returns when I first started the process.  I was then asked by Better for permission through a 3rd party to pull my tax returns from the IRS (which I refused).  Instead, I was asked to sign an IRS form giving permission to pull my tax returns (for the years I had already uploaded).  Lastly, It was a bit of a blur, but I'm pretty sure I signed the same IRS form during my closing also.  At some point, you have the information.  You make me uncomfortable when you keep asking for the same sensitive items repeatedly.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thank you, Mick!

reviewDescription: By far the smoothest home finance/refinance process EVER! Excellent communication and collaboration between team members. I even got a call on a Sunday afternoon regarding a question I had emailed about. I mean, how many banks are open on a Sunday afternoon?! And of course they have unbeatable rates. I highly recommend them.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thanks Maggie.

reviewDescription: Super simple refi and closed quick! They had the best closing costs and rates also
reviewCompanyResponse: Thank you for the review, Daniel!

reviewDescription: At the beginning things felt very rushed. Information that was sent to us didn’t match what you’ll had and I was confused until Molli called and spoke with us personally. She was very patient, easy to talk to, explained the process clearly and answered our questions with kindness. She built good rapport with us and that was super important to us. After that, things seemed to flow very well and the communication with her and Brian was great. Brian was also very patient and kind throughout the process. Being able to send text messages with Brian was so super helpful for us. It gave us peace of mind that he knew we were doing our best to get our tasks done timely and he responded quickly to us. We feel that Molli and Brian both strived to make this work for us and we got a better deal than we expected. You’ll were recommended by a friend who also refinanced with you’ll and we too will also recommend you’ll to our friends and family. We sincerely appreciate all the hard work and long hours Molli and Brian put in to help us get this done. Back in September of last year, we had started the process with another provider but it fell through. I always say that things happen for a reason. You’ll gave us a much better rate and we had a great experience. We have absolutely no complaints whatsoever about how our case was handled. In fact we are wholeheartedly appreciative and thank you’ll from the bottom of our hearts. Molli and Brian are both amazing assets to your company and we truly hope they are recognized for their work.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thank you for the review.

reviewTitle: It was flawless and the customer…

I am being funded today so I can't wait to verify that it is all a done deal.  Thank you Better!  You certainly are!

Enrico Medina

reviewTitle: As a first-timer refinancing a home
reviewDescription:
As a first-timer refinancing a home, it was the best decision I have ever made. From initial application to closing, Better Mortgage provided full support and outstanding customer service. Better Mortgage did not leave me any doubts at all. The level of professionalism was clearly their bread and butter; being honest and professional, answering any questions promptly, and most importantly, keeping me updated for any changes.

Thank you Better for moving your customers one step closer to making their lives better.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thanks, Gregory!

reviewTitle: Had a nightmare ordeal with them
reviewDescription: Had a nightmare ordeal with them. They have you do tasks. Everyday I would deal with these tasks (upload documents) and EVERY morning there would be twice as many tasks than the ones I did the previous days. We own a small business and it took me explaining with every task the difference between our corporation name and our DBA (Doing Business As) name, emailing them about it for over a month before they kinda got it. They kept asking for the tax returns for the DBA 🙃 it’s the same as the corporation. It was extremely stressful dealing with the non stop issues and tasks. I do not recommend this at all. If you want to ReFi, call the bank who has your loan first. They request the documents once and leave you alone till they need your signatures to close the loan. NEVER EVER WILL I DEAL WITH THESE PEOPLE AGAIN! You think it will be so easy and less evasive, trust me, I was never so wrong about this.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hey Colleen, I'm so sorry to hear about this, but thank you for letting us know. Please reach out to support@better.com if you'd like to discuss further.

reviewDescription: Overall, this is absolutely the best mortgage process that we have ever undertaken. You provided 6-Star service up until the closing phase, during which we think the closing agent should have been more responsive to questions we asked (we had to repeat several questions multiple times before we got a response, and never got answers to several others) and worked to support the actual closing when more questions arose on some of the closing documents (was on a Saturday when he was scheduled off; there were errors on some of the closing documents that he prepared). We are not trying to throw the closing agent under the bus, but we were prepared to switch to another lender when we could not get responses to our questions. Usually these kinds of issues can be resolved with training and supervision, and everyone else on your team were stellar, professional, and phenomenally responsive. We will recommend your services to our friends and family members; in fact, we already have and our son closed before we did!
reviewCompanyResponse: Thanks Steve for the honest review!

reviewDescription: One of the best experiences I’ve ever had!
reviewCompanyResponse: Thank you, Mark.

reviewDescription: This was my first time having a great experience with a mortgage company out of four earlier experiences, both buying homes and refinancing. We were able to close on our refi in a timely manner, and in our own home - in the evening. I will always highly recommend this company and each representative that we interacted with as the most professional and knowledgeable individuals.

reviewDescription:
My Great Experience with Better Mortgage

    Another important thing - My rate was locked in the beginning so I was not surprised on closing.

reviewTitle: The best lender in the States
reviewDescription: Everything right form pre -approval is online. It is ideal for tech savvy who wants to do everything online. You have a team dedicated to you and you can decide the amount of  interaction you want to have. If you can understand everything from the instructions  and have no questions, there is no need to talk with any loan officer or whoever. What a breeze! That is the part which I liked the most. However, I would suggest an improvement. Every time a task becomes available, there should be a notification to the registered email.  Most of the time  I do get an email about available tasks. However, the email notification   should be 100%. I always have the account logged in and I did it instantaneously. Overall, I am 100%satisfied with Better mortgage and would happily recommend to friends and family. There is no origination fee and they have the lowest interest rate in the entire US. I shopped with nearly 5 lenders before choosing Better mortgage and I am very  happy I chose them.

The process was truly painless, seamless, and so easy!

reviewDescription:
i am pleased to take this opportunity to express how i felt throughout the Refinance Process.
Initially the pressure was very high regarding the documents required to be submitted. Although we felt this pressure the staff guiding us were very calm and focused.
Subsequently everything worked so wonderful when we understood the process.
The continuous and timely guidance helped significantly.
We ended the process very satisfied and we hope to continue with Better Mortgage into the future. Also we are ready and willing to refer Better Mortgage to friends and relatives.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thanks Edwin!

reviewDescription:
Smooth sailing for a straightforward loan.  Very easy experience from application to closing.  All of the associates I worked with via email and phone were courteous and professional, efficiently providing me with the necessary help needed.  Loan was approved very quickly.  Even my realtor commented multiple times on how smooth the transaction was.  While my experience was very good, I can see why one size does not fit all: 
1. The amount of documentation required for a mortgage can seem overwhelming for some, and having to do it all yourself online without immediate feedback can cause issues.  Also, my loan application was very clean avoiding many scenarios that may not be optimally solved without face to face interaction.
2. Better.com associates are extremely overloaded.  Emails took days to answer, and sometimes calendars for booking a phone call appeared completely booked.  
3. Multiple points of contact during the process.

Will not hesitate to use Better.com for any future mortgage needs.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thanks Bradley!

reviewTitle: It was a very straightforward and…
reviewDescription: It was a very straightforward and painless process to get our refinance done with Better. Everything in the user portal was well laid out, the tasks were easy to follow, and the team members were quick and easy to communicate with as well as being very knowledgeable in their areas of focus. If we ever need to refinance or get another mortgage I anticipate that we will do it with Better.

reviewDescription: The process was extremely easy, quick and effortless.  I would definitely recommend better.com for refinancing purposes.

reviewTitle: This whole experience was a pleasure

This whole experience was a pleasure. Everyone I talked with or emailed with was very polite, professional and informative. I also loved being able to do the closing on our dining room table. I wasn't actively looking to refinance but a coworker of mine referred me to your site and I'm glad I did.

If I was going to nit pick something is that sometimes your website says there are 2 task to do, but when I click on the task tab there are none listed to do. This is really a minor website issue but in general, your website is very user friendly.

Thanks again for a really easy process and I'm recommending you to anyone looking for a mortgage.

reviewTitle: Great system and the team members were…
reviewDescription: Great system and the team members were very helpful. Sometimes the system was a bit rigid in accepting documents but not a big hurdle. Would reccomend.

reviewTitle: Great experience from start to finish
reviewDescription: Great experience from start to finish.  Seamless process, and all of the staff were very helpful!

reviewTitle: Good experience for people comfortable with DIY/online portals.
reviewDescription: Overall good experience. I like how the "tasks" in your account are clearly visible when you need to complete something new. All the customer service people I spoke with were very nice. Only minor complaints would be the section where you chat back and forth with questions in your account says they will get back to you in 24 hours but they did not (but always eventually did). Also the wait time when calling in was quite long.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thank you Scott!

reviewTitle: Very simple online platform to manage…
reviewDescription: Very simple online platform to manage the various steps and needs throughout the process. Perfect for those who don't have a ton of free time to dedicate to the home purchasing process. Absolutely no complaints!
reviewCompanyResponse: Thanks Devin!

reviewTitle: UX was A++, some of the questions could be improved though.
reviewDescription: The user experience online is awesome! Quick turnaround on basically every interaction. Reminded me a lot of online tax prep guided questioning. Loved and appreciated the speediness of everything and everyone. There was one area that I think could be improved, and that was the questions that can't be undone. There was a question asking the address of "my home" so I gave it my current address. Turns out, the question really wanted the address of the house I was looking to purchase (which I don't yet consider to be "my home"). I realized that I'd glazed over the fine print on the question, which specified... so definitely my fault. However, this turned out to be a HUGE problem for us, because the answer to that question, once submitted, updated a bunch of other questions. The agent couldn't undo my answer, and I ultimately had to start the ENTIRE application process all over, and I was pretty far into it at this point. So maybe for questions like that, which have broad impact on the rest of the application, add an additional "you sure?" type dialog, and consider updating the wording so that dum-dums like myself who sometimes skip fine print can't screw it up so easily ;) lol.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thank you Jake!

reviewDescription: Everyone in the process was wonderfully helpful and got back to me right away.  This has been the easiest home loan we have ever had to get.  Thanks for making it easy so that we could do it from home and with little headache!
reviewCompanyResponse: Thanks Ginny!

reviewTitle: The team at Better.com was amazing!
reviewDescription: The team at Better.com was amazing to work with. Both Ryan Dennis and Teal Metzner were awesome and very quick to respond to questions. There are no complaints here and will most likely use Better.com in the future.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thank you for the review, Doug!

reviewDescription: The experience with Better was a pleasure from start to finish.  The website is easy and intuitive.  Each person I dealt with throughout the process was courteous and easy to reach.  The only part of the process that was troubling was when it came time to actually wire the money.  Better did a pretty good job of prepping me for what to expect, but that didn't change the fact I was a little nervous sending a big wire transfer out without being completely certain it was not some type of scam.  Obviously my fears were unfounded.  Everything went through fine and I can now say with certainty that my experience with Better was 100% great.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thanks, Hank!

reviewDescription: Very easy refinancing my house. Rates were competitive and the staff was very helpful. Would definitely recommend to a friend
reviewCompanyResponse: Thanks, Edgar!

reviewTitle: Best product and customer service out there! Three loans to date!
reviewDescription: Better has been by far, the best mortgage experience for us. We have finished three loans with them and plan to do one more. The product and the team is the perfect blend of technology and customer service. I enjoy wholeheartedly, recommending them to friends and family.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thanks Andrea!

reviewTitle: We really enjoyed all of the process
reviewDescription: We really enjoyed all of the process. It was easy and happened quickly without delay. I’m not sure it was an inexpensive as advertised but it was worth it.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thank you for the honest review, Audrey!

reviewDescription: Overall things went smoothly. There were a couple of documents that I had to print, sign and scan. And there was an oversight by the closing agent/Notary that required a scanned document after we initialed it, but other than that pretty smooth. I commend the people and the process.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thanks Chris!

reviewDescription: Worked on refinancing 2 loans simultaneously. Even though we have no payment issues and great credit, Fannie Mae guidelines did not allow us to count some income. As a result, we only managed to refinance 1 property for now. We had an excellent team at Better helping with the process. Special thanks to Stephanie Flores and her manager Patrick for pushing the process along and not giving up when underwriting was pushing back with extra requests. Will be back to refi 2nd property in a little while.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thanks Vadim!

reviewTitle: Great experience, with some suggestions for improving further
reviewDescription:
Generally speaking, love the platform that Better has built to simplify the byzantine process of qualifiying for a mortgage as well as meeting the reporting requirements.  The engine up front for estimating differnt loan configurations and automatically generating prequal letter was the best I saw during the shopping process.  Also the process of receiving "tasks" from the loan team was straightforward.  

Only a few suggestions.  One...would be nice to have a better idea of what paperwork is required further on in the process, in many cases it would have been easier just to tell me up front what you'd need so I can compile it all at once.  Second, I'm sure you understand that making the out of pocket for down payment and closing costs is a mad dash to generate liquidity (ie cash).  So...if I'm going to have to sell a car, or ask a relative for a gift, or even do an early withdrawal from my retirement, you should let me know up front what the paperwork is that you'll need (e.g., take a picture of that car title!).  Finally, the video course on buying a home was REALLY helpful, but should be assigned to the borrower as a task much earlier on.  

However, overall it was a good though stressful experience, and would recommend you to others.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thank you Ashley!

reviewDescription: One more thing- reading Trustpilot, they have no record of Better asking customers to review them here yet this is exactly what Better did with me.

Overall, Better did an absolutely fantastic job handling our loan. Our processing expert, Micheal Squilante, was readily available and knowledgeable. He answered my questions and emails in a timely fashion and worked diligently on our behalf to close ON TIME, which I couldn't appreciate more.

There were two items that made the process give me a little anxiety. 

#1. Our home was being built in a rural area where the county and the city did not do a certificate of occupancy or a municipal inspection report. While our processing expert offered many items we could produce in lieu of this, there was no clear, defined documents outlined as possibilities. I don't think the underwriting team told him exactly what we needed to offer so we collected as much random permit/inspection information as possible and submitted it. It would have made me feel a lot better had we been given a clear list of items that would have worked instead as it was all left up to hoping what we did provide would be good enough. That made me feel as if there were a lot of unknowns at the end and the possibility of our file being returned by underwriting at the last minute was not ideal considering all the planning we had to do for moving.

#2. I'll preface this by saying that our closing expert handled everything quickly, and he clearly knew what he was doing. It all came down to the wire in the end, but it did get handled and there were no mistakes. However, the communication at this point was lacking. There were several emails not replied to and I had to call to get updates. When it's the last few days before closing, this is not ideal and caused no small amount of anxiety about having to change movers, installers, ect around. While he did a fantastic job getting the closing over the finish line, I felt like my emails were going into the abyss and it left me feeling unsure about whether or not things were going to happen as scheduled. 

As I said, overall the experience was a great one and Better gave me a great rate, great closing costs and the web portal was incredibly easy to use. Not sure if banks do something similar these days, but being able to upload everything was quick and easy and being able to easily check my loan estimate made this whole thing much less stressful than emailing documents back and forth wondering if I've provided all that was needed.
reviewCompanyResponse: Thank you for the review!

I also would have appreciated a flow chart or similar visual that showed the steps of the entire process with time frames--a big picture summary.  All of the individuals that I had contact with through the process were pleasant, knowledgeable and responded in a timely manner to my e mails or phone messages.  Overall, the experience was a positive one.

reviewDescription:

reviewTitle: Efficient and time saving low head ache way of refi
reviewDescription: This is the 2nd time we refinanced with Better Mortgage (1st happened quick and easy in summer of 2020). We found Better on nerdwallet where it's ranked A+. 1st mortgage refi with Better was a breeze and done in 3 weeks (it took one week to find a notary public willing to visit a remote area). If we'd asked for a notary in town it'd be a 15 day refi, literally. It's hard to believe it was a breeze as I assess our financial situation as complicated involving more paperwork than a straightforward refi. The 2nd refi was a challenge and it took 3 tries to make it happen. 1st refi try went nowhere because it was discovered that there was a million dollar lawsuit against our HOA, and no bank would touch that. 2nd refi attempt, the lawsuit was thrown out but we had to abort the mission due to a family disagreement (California's brute handling of the virus been tough on folks in the gold state). Better understood no questions asked.  The 3rd time's the charm refi just finally happened, but took over 2 months. The reason was something else... Our HELOC bank has 1 star [out of 5] rating and it was slowing us down with their 21 day response policies (our original HELOC was sold to that horror bank). The nightmare bank's HELOC "mortgage" had to be what's called subordinated to the new refi loan (duckduckgo what loan subordination is). Better finally got what we wanted out of the hell bank.  Better are the STELLAR amongst the good the bad and the ugly. They go above and beyond. We've locked into the lowest rate set to expire April 5, '21 yet the rate was honored until April 17 when we signed. I've seen what an old school refi is with the use of fax and "you must get" A to get B, but you can't get A unless you already got B mumbo-jumbo and reps spacing out when you got to leave messages to the guy and his manager and their director to make things budge. Better Mortgage took the nonsense and the runaround out of refi. Now's the time to refi- because negative interest rates are VERY unlikely in US. And just think of it- if that was to happen YOU'd pay a bank some interest just for keeping your $ in the bank! I don't think there'll be better time to refi as you'll get the same thing next year at best- so why stagnate with paying your ok bank more for longer??? Aim at getting done with mortgage for good. The myth that you'll save a ton on taxes writing interest off is just a myth. Think how much you'll save when you pay your place off and there's no more paying to the big brother??? Picture this: No more mortgage payments! Go for shorter term- you save a ton!  Worst thing that will happen you will create a bunch of online accounts to download forms, or just install a free document scanner app and scan away those 20-40 documents or less they'll ask all in all and next thing you know- the rifi is done! You'll look at your new mortgage statement and see how fat principal rapidly goes skinny! Don't sit on it! Don't skip a beat and Better.com won't skip one! Go for it! If you do like 1040EZ taxes (or close to that) all mighty himself wants you to refi like yesterday as it's EZ quick, all painless, and simple!  ;) And here's one cute detail for ya: I did not mind taking an hour out of my vacation to sign papers on Hawaii - was done at a mall dining area during CoViD. Yep, Better will make it happen for you if you don't mind. My vacation got a whole lot better because we'd signed on the Big Island :)
reviewCompanyResponse: Thank you, Michael!

Strung Along and then denied
My experience with this internet based mortgage company was very unsatisfactory. After several weeks of supplying an endless list of documents for a refinance of a single family private residence and being self employed, my loan application was denied. The denial was simply on the basis that my business (lodging) lost money due to Covid state travel restrictions in 2020 after over 20 years of generating significant annual net income over $100,000. My credit, loan to value, payment history, assets (over several million) were all very strong as they acknowledged. The extremely disappointing aspect of this interaction is that they did not tell me that I would not qualify up front which would have saved considerable wasted effort on my part. Their approach to assigning tasks for obtaining information at first appeared efficient and doable except after completing the first batch of tasks, more were assigned. The task process seemed to go on endlessly with no end in sight. As I said, a simple inperson interview could have avoided all the wasted effort on my part. They operate very decentralized with a very impersonal approach. I definitely would not recommend dealing with Better Mortgage as in my experience they did not live up in any way to their company name.

reviewTitle: Terrible experience.  RSU income? Read this
reviewDescription:
I had the worst experience I ever had refinancing a mortgage with Better.com.  First the whole automated site may work unless you actually need to talk to anyone.  They constantly ping you with "tasks" many times asking for the same information.  I didn't find any of the actual people I worked with knowledgeable and they have the most inept and incompetent underwriting department you would have the displeasure of working with.

But the main issue was how they qualify RSU income.   First they told me they don't qualify RSU as income.  I complained and fought with them and they didn't budge. Then I found an actual page on their own site saying they do qualify the income! Once I sent them their own page they relented and qualified the income.

The biggest joke came when they described how they qualified.  They used the least amount of stock vested in a 3 year period and randomly chose a stock price anywhere from the previous 2 years. Anyone working in a tech company knows they typically grant RSU's yearly that vest in a 4 year cycle which means in year 3 your stocks would be at or near the lowest level because that's your furthest vest date.  Long story short they ended up qualifying .18% of my total RSU grant!  That's not a typo.

Please stay far away from this company and find a company that's actually qualified to do their job.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hi EG, we're so sorry to hear about the experience but appreciate the honest review. Please feel free to reach out to our Client Success Team at support@better.com to discuss further.

reviewTitle: Strung Along, and Hung Out to Dry
reviewDescription: Better Mortgage offers Fannie Mae loans. I was advised by the screening loan officer that I was a great candidate for that loan. We discussed my circumstances in detail, including all obstacles for this type of loan. He said that I was fine, but that he would run it by his team to make sure. Everything seemed fine, and so we went through the whole process for four weeks, with a costly appraisal. The loan fell through because of items that we thoroughly examined before I committed to the process. It really messed things up. I qualify for a home equity loan, but four weeks had gone by, and I had a 30 day window to get financing. Better Mortgage lacks respect for the client.

Better.com representatives would not take any action against the incorrect appraisal and refused to oblige to any requests I was making to validate the appraisal or to order a new one.

reviewDescription:
HOMEOWNERS BEWARE!  
*tldr*- Once you pay the inspection fee, they string you along till they have a reason to cancel your account!

Better.com is one of the worst companies I have had the displeasure of working with!  We tried to refinance our home at the start of October 2020.  We happily supplied all the required documents when prompted to on their website.  After paying the $500 for an appraisal on our house (btw the appraiser never showed up or walked in the house) all communication with actual people stopped.  Each month we were requested to send them proof of mortgage being paid, proof of income, and proof of retirement account balance.  Each month they would keep our account in limbo and continue to push back the closing date with no word on why.  Around the end of February of 2021 (A WHOLE 6 MONTHS AFTER STARTING THIS PROCESS!) our account on better.com just vanished.  No calls, emails, texts... nothing.  I call in a panic to see if its a problem on my end why I cant get into my account and the person says that there is no account under my phone number.  I luckily found an email at the start with our account id and gave it to the representative and she asked me to verify the phone number on the account.  I could not... the number they had in their system was a number I have never seen before.  I then verified ownership of the account with my social and she then informed me that the account was closed because I had not supplied them with the requested documents in 3 days, so they closed my account.  Furious that I had spent 6 months giving them what they asked for each month and never getting notified that there was an action needed before terminating my account, I asked to speak with my account manager to see what can be done.  Gareth Cobb (my account manager) was currently in a meeting and could not talk to me so I was told he would reach out as soon as he gets off the phone.  She then changed the number on the account to match my real phone number (important detail later).  So I left the call around 10am expecting Gareth Cobb to call me back shortly.  That never happened...  Come the next day I called in again.  The representative asked me to verify my phone number again and I told her my number... it did not match the number on file.  I then gave her the number I had no clue whose it was and she said that was the phone number on the account!!  I explained that Gareth Cobb was supposed to call me the previous day about my account but he never called.  She tried to send me over to him but he again was in a meeting and said he will call me back later.  I stupidly believed that I was going to talk to my account manager so I hung up.  No call came in... it was also Friday so I would not get an answer for another 2 days at least.  When Monday rolled around I called them first thing and this time I was actually upset.  The representative was nice and sympathetic and nice but again my account manager Gareth Cobb could not be reached and would have to call me back.  I asked her more questions this time and was told that Gareth Cobb will defiantly be reaching out to me by the end of the day.  I also said I want to speak to a manager, to which she said a manager will call me shortly about my account.
Well... two weeks later and neither one has called.  

So now I am out the $500 for the inspection, this whole thing feels like a total SCAM.
-John Baxter

reviewTitle: Low rates, good interface, should've been much better.
reviewDescription:
I'm sure others might have had better experiences with their applications, but I had a horrible and lengthy process with Better.

Everything was great in the beginning. Locked in a good rate. Website was easy to use. Seems like it'd be straightforward.

But then the underwriting process happened. My contact was unresponsive at times, emails would go unanswered. Apparently the lead underwriter took leave at some point, and others took over. They were not well informed of my case, and made me verify various documents over and over again. Pretty unorganized. They also claim 24/7 support. Don't hold your breath. 

Eventually, I was approved for the loan, and even then, closing was such a pain. Since actual closing date was delayed by almost a month (their end), they required an amended purchase contract despite both my lawyer and theirs saying that it wasn't required. We were left waiting at the attorney's office, on closing day, for hours while waiting for funds to be released (it wasn't btw, but we figured out how to close without).

tl;dr: good luck.

Update:
Will be closing with Semper home loans in 2 days. Forget better.com and go with Semper. 
After replying to the better.com complaint Dept, all that came of it was "nothing we can do". 
9 years ago I filed for bankruptcy and all debts were discharged including the condo that was forclosed on. The bank that forclosed on the condo decided to complete the forclosure 4 years after the bankruptcy. (Unbeknownst to me). To date all my bills are paid on time including my current mortgage of 4 years. Better.com required proof that I sold the property which obviously I can not provide since I lost ownership of the condo because it was included in the bankruptcy. 
So as far as I'm concerned, better.com could have made an exception. I'm sure if I had connections in high places my refinance would have been completed  So screw better.com and their pencil neck underwriters.

reviewTitle: My experience with better.com was not good...
reviewDescription:
I engaged Better.com to complete a refinance on my home after doing significant research into mortgage companies online. I was sold on their marketing, and was excited for a new, "better" mortgage experience. I found the online portal to be fast, easy, and user-friendly, but the results to be far from that.


Background:
- Better.com representatives stated I was eligible for a refinance based on my home's value, along with LTV & CLTV
- I inquired three times on recorded lines, as I was not sure myself based on my own research
- They confirmed that I was in fact eligible, and I proceeded with an application


After 20+ hours of my own work, digging up countless personal and financial documents for them, paying $550 for an appraisal, letting an appraiser into my home during a pandemic, hard credit checks, and getting all the way to underwriting and title review did they realize the issue, that I was never eligible for the loan based on the agreed upon parameters, and I would need to come up with over $80K due to Better.com's mistake (even though we had discussed this exact scenario during the initial calls).


A manager for better.com confirmed on two separate calls that some sort of "foul play" took place. I have stopped trying to assume what that was or why...


I have tried to reach out to leadership via multiple means and have even had a better.com employee refuse to forward emails to leadership. For now, my complaint is apparently going nowhere but here and to the Consumer Fraud Protection Bureau.


Make your own decisions as my experience may not be reflective of yours.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hey JR, so sorry to hear about the frustration during your experience but we appreciate the honest review. We are sending this feedback to our team. If you'd like to discuss further, please reach out to our Client Success Team at complaints@better.com.

reviewTitle: Save your time and find another lender
reviewDescription:
I was referred to better.com by a realtor with a very positive endorsement of a quick and easy refinance.  My initial experience with the websites was as expected.

The reality is that I wasted a bunch of time and effort providing their underwriting process with document after document.

Once I was approved, I was expecting to move to closing within a few weeks.  First sign of trouble was that the Title which was supposedly ordered in December was delayed, and they had to reorder in January.

After waiting around on that for 4 weeks, the loan finally cleared Title.

Well, here we are after 2 months and they are asking me for updates to the documents that I already sent them because they are too far beyond their guidelines.

I was about to concede and send them additional documents and within minutes they send me an email stating that because the County I live in has been declared a disaster area due to the recent winter storm, I'm going to need to obtain a property inspection.

I'm sure all of these things really are underwriting guidelines, but they wasted a bunch of time with their incompetence.

They really did give me a good rate and I'm sorry that I was unable to take advantage of it.

I'm done with these guys and I would not recommend wasting your time either.

reviewDescription:
Steer clear - this place is a disaster.  After 3 weeks of submitting endless amounts of information, I’m told that I don’t have enough income to qualify on a 420k refinance with a 35%LTV, 841 FICO, and 2.6m liquid assets.

Baffled, I asked to see how they’re computing my income (most is passive income from rental properties) - 80% of it was missing and they didn’t say a word about it.

Assuming it was a misunderstanding, I detailed the income, including fully executed lease agreements.  They rebutted, stating the income isn’t listed on my 2019 tax return - and that they must follow FNMA procedures for documenting income.

I pointed out that the properties in question were purchased in Q4 2019 so it would be impossible to show income from them on my 2019 tax returns and instead submitted fully executed lease agreements, copies of all income from 2020, along with a reference to the FNMA guidelines that allow the documentation I submitted for this exact situation.

Their response: “ The scenario question you posed is not able to be considered because it does not pertain to the situation on this loan. ”

What?  It’s 80% of my income, of course it pertains to this loan.

Long story short - their underwriting department has no idea what they’re doing.  As a former mortgage broker for over 15 years, I’ve never seen such incompetence and arrogance in a lender.  Don’t buy the teaser rates, this place is a joke.

For those of you that have passive rental income, here’s the very FNMA guidelines they say they follow:

Reconciling Partial or No Rental History on Tax Returns

In order for the lender to determine qualifying rental income, the lender must determine whether or not the rental property was in service for the entire tax year or only a portion of the year. In some situations, the lender’s analysis may determine that using alternative rental income calculations or using lease agreements to calculate income are more appropriate methods for calculating the qualifying income from rental properties. This policy may be applied to refinances of a subject rental property or to other rental properties owned by the borrower.

If the borrower is able to document (per the table below) that the rental property was not in service the previous tax year, or was in service for only a portion of the previous tax year, the lender may determine qualifying rental income by using

Schedule E income and expenses, and annualizing the income (or loss) calculation; or
fully executed lease agreement(s) to determine the gross rental income to be used in the net rental income (or loss) calculation.
Better.com has turned the traditional painful mortgage experience into a task oriented, self service frictionless experience. I’m a fan

We had already been officially approved and were only days before closing. This had nothing to do with our credit, home appraisals, or financial situation. They said they were very sorry for the miscommunications. 

Unfortunately while waiting on them to change their mind, rates have gone up almost half a percent and the lock I have them is worthless. Their cheaper rate has cost me tens of thousands of dollars. Yes, the published rates are excellent, but they will do you no good if they decide they don’t want your loan at the last second. 

Applied: 11/12/20
Rate Lock: 11/25/20
Sent to Underwriting: 12/8/20
Approved: 1/14/20
Rescinded: 1/19/20

reviewDescription: I have financed about 6 properties so far with big banks (PNC etc.) and even with a 810 credit score the loan process with Better.com was hands down the worst.The rates might be lower but the overall application process is painful. Hundreds of tasks are automatically generated with a lot being duplicates. You will be uploading unnecessary documents on a daily basis with no end in sight. Other banks have a contact person who will ask for a specific set of documents - not with Better.com. A computer software essentially comes up with a list on a daily basis. Even after the closing documents were signed - better.com required additional documents. And the fun didn't stop after closing. Within 30 days of the closing - the loan servicer was changed without my permission - payments made to the first servicer was not reflected in the information sent to the second. We were left scrambling to get the 2 working together with late fee and adverse credit reporting implications. My advice - stick to the organizations where you can meet a person face to face. That human touch is important in these transactions. Also stick to organizations that have a certain level of certainty around who will be servicing the loan.

We refinanced a house in Portland OR over the Christmas / New Years period (when there was very high mortgage demand) in 31 days.

Better has no "origination" or "underwriting" fees, and their staff do not operate on commission so there is none of the used-car-salesmanship you can get from other lenders.

Cons:
You can end up dealing with a number of different people (3+) as each stage of the loan process is handled by a different specialist.

We did receive a couple strange "Appraisal has been ordered" notifications despite being given an appraisal waiver - which the team seemed to think had been sent in error. I had to organize all those email correspondences and pointed out and circled dates those documents were sent electronically. There're no responses at times.
Then, a loan processor was finally introduced in mid-October. We'd been playing with required paperwork forever.

After over 93 days, I began to question why I had been asked the same documents which were submitted. No response or instruction was received. I was left with puzzlement up in the air and even brought my case to the managerial level. Nothing's changed. Finally, after 113 days in end of November, 2020, I became alert that I'm getting nowhere near the closing process in such pace. I began to greatly wonder if such paperwork game was only an excuse because no funding was available yet. Title search was nowhere in view, and my refinance was far off.

Therefore, I started searching new lenders and picked 2 to start all over again, but this time I'd already wasted almost 4 months and must quickly get a loan before reaching my mortgage loan maturity date. Thank God for giving me a company named Better which was an exact opposite to Amerisave. I was assigned a loan officer from the very beginning of the loan process. Everyday I was working closely with the loan officer and guided clearly what to be submitted step by step steadily toward the end. In 38 days, closing was done (might have been shorter term if Christmas and New Year didn't happen in between), and my loan was finally funded with acknowledgment today.

With Better, I was covered precisely what was going to take place. Attentiveness, involvement, organization, promptness, and success are the experience I got from Better. Isn't that a contrast to what I had experienced with the first interested lender!

I want to share my experience with others who try to obtain a mortgage loan and hope sincerely that they're able to get help from the best service provider.

reviewTitle: Would recommend to refinance somewhere else
reviewDescription:
The efficiency of the marketing team, property appraisal and underwriting was impressive. However, the impressiveness stops there. I had utilized "better  mortgage" as well as "better settlement services" for title. The closing was not smooth at all. The closing agent assigned was giving me scripted responses rather than based on my actual case (eg, when I asked how to make payment for the cash to close, got a scripted response that it will depend on my title company. My title company had already been finalized to better settlement services itself and we were awaiting for the final closing disclosure! I would have expected the agent to look at my case and respond based on their own title company's process! A little effort would have helped here.)
For the closing itself, I was provided a set of possible dates based on better mortgage and better settlement's feasibility and I was required to pick the date and time. When I finalized the date and time of closing, better.com was not able to provide me with the closing disclosure till the time of closing! Had to get it rescheduled. Even at that rescheduled time, the notary was given multiple versions of the closing disclosure rather than only the final version adding to confusion.

Finally, the funding for my existing mortgage payoff did not happen on the date that was provided by better.com itself. When contacted, the blame for the delay was pointed to the title company (which again is better settlement service which is a service offered in better.com). The title company when contacted responded next day that they received the funding from the lender (better mortgage) a day after the scheduled funding and the funding is in queue and put a disclaimer that they don't have timelines and they are a bit backed up, but will get to it asap. This was a bit stressful as I was not sure if I was supposed to stop my scheduled mortgage payment to my previous mortgage company nor was I sure what will happen if the funding delay is greater than a week as I did not get clarity from the closing agent.

Finally, the funding to my prior mortgage company happened with 3 days of delay.

Now I am charged interest for the same mortgage amount at two different places for those 3 days. Better told they would refund, when I had raised it during the delay of closing. Post that, they did not take any action and I had to follow up on it. Right now, they have not given me any clarity how or when it would be refunded!


Not sure if this is the usual head ache of refinancing at other mortgage companies. One thing for sure, better.com did not make refinancing easy. I see lot of people sharing positive experiences. At least, that was not the case with me nor will I use or recommend better.com to anyone else.

reviewTitle: This company runs an elegant scam
reviewDescription: This company runs an elegant scam. Here's how the scam works. They first look at your credit score (mine was over 800) and tell you that you will have no problem qualifying for a mortgage. After, you must submit a lot of paperwork on a site that does not work. It keeps asking for the same information over and over again. I sent these people my tax returns so they knew exactly the amount I wanted to borrow (which was approved) and they also knew my income from completed tax returns for 2018 and 2019. I had already had an appraisal done on my property which came in at $1 million+. They said however that they could only give me a mortgage if I used their appraiser and paid $550. An appraiser came to my house and spent less than 10 minutes looking around. The first appraisal I had done cost $300 and the appraiser spent over 2 hours making accurate measurements, etc.) Their appraiser appraised my property at $150,000 less than the appraisal I had for a licensed real estate appraiser and over $150,000 less than than the Zillow estimated value. After sending them more documents they finally said that they could not do the mortgage because the income to debt ratio was too low!! But, they knew this before they asked me to pay for an additional useless an inaccurate appraisal. In my opinion, they probably pay the appraiser half of the $550 (or less?) and keep the change. The scam is simple. Convince borrowers to get appraisals, let borrowers waste their time uploading documents on their poorly organized site, then tell the borrow that they can't do the mortgage because of weak excuses. Do not use this company!!!

Because I cannot speak publicly about the specifics of your situation, a member of our Client Success Team will be contacting you directly. Sincerely, the Better Team.

reviewTitle: Overall experience was great, there were hiccups.
reviewDescription:
I have seen a lot of reviews out there that mention the experience being terrible, long and not working out. I'd take that with a grain of salt, as a lot of those seem to be by people that didn't fully qualify or later found out that there were issues with their finances. We used Better for a mortgage on our house.

Overall our experience was very good. Our loan was issued on time and our rate was ridiculously good! I actually shopped it around for over a week and no one could beat it. Their website is VERY user friendly and accomplishing tasks is very easy. That is not to say, however, that there weren't hiccups along the way. We had some issues during appraisal, processing, and closing. reviewDescription: Very seamless refi, communication was always swift (as in minutes/hours) unlike other lenders days/weeks. My only complaint was after my appraisal they adjusted my loan because my home didn’t come in at a 70% LTV, it came in at 79%. This was never explained to me and would have cost me $1000 more. I explained this was not disclosed and they restored my original terms (D on initial but A on resolution). Second issue was I was told my appraisal fee of $550 would be refunded at closing. It was not in the disclosure so I asked several times through the process and was told I would get a check after closing for the fee. Closing came and I asked when they would send the $550, they said I would get $100 as the appraisal was 450. They said I would get a refund if the home didn’t require an appraisal (not what I was told and not what was disclosed to me). I argued and they relented and are suppose to be sending my $550. (D on initial disclosure, A on resolution). People are friendly and seems like a great company....but, they do not service the loan (semi-normal). So, feel safe with using Better but read your documents, understand them and make sure they disclose all fees, if they don’t argue the fact and they should make things right.

reviewDescription:
Great experience overall.  I found it easy to reach people through phone or e-mail - all were very helpful and efficient.  Their online system for entering information was easy to use and the initial process was much easier than with other companies.  I would definitely use them again and recommend them to others.
Negatives were that you had to check the documents carefully as they changed often.  The problem I had was with a second home that also is rented out and ended up being classified as an investment property, which caused an increase in the interest rate and almost caused me to bail.  We were able to work it out and I have to say everyone I talked to in at least three states were easy to reach and helpful.  The initial estimate on Nerdwallet was not what came up when I went farther, and that was a little annoying (in contrast to another company where the Nerdwallet offer was identical to what they had).  Once in their system, the Better.com interface allowed you to update often and then you could lock in when the terms were right - this was a great advantage, with another company you were locked in right at the beginning.  Better.com also has the best refinance calculator I have seen - it allows you to compare the benefits from a reduced interest rate or loan term even if your payments don't increase.  I found this a great help if you are interested in refinancing to increase net wealth in the future rather than simply lowering monthly payments.  Overall, a great company and I recommend them.

reviewTitle: Fast, overall experience was as good as could be expected.
reviewDescription:
Refinancing in Texas (with our weird real estate laws)- Better was great throughout the process. It took about five weeks overall. 

Their “digital-native” platform is great for busy Millennials (or others :) ) such as myself who don’t have time to navigate a ton of phone trees to speak with someone, and haven’t seen a fax machine in years to submit docs (other banks are somehow still doing business this way!)

Better assigns a team of people for three phases of the process- a 
“Mortgage Expert”, who helps with initial review up until rate lock; a 
“Processing Expert”, who shepherds the loan through underwriting and doc verifications; and a 
“Closing Expert” who takes it from underwriting to Close. 

My team was great, though the title company suggested was a bit of a mess (I could have comparison-shopped for that if I’d wanted to though).

I’d also like to mention that I was refinancing amidst the Fannie/Freddie “0.5% Market Risk Fee” announcement. I’d locked my rate with that fee implicitly included, but after it was announced it wouldn’t apply to any loans until December 2020 (I closed in Sept), Better.com emailed to mention I should expect a check with a refund for 0.5% of my loan amount! It would’ve been very easy (and expected) for them to just keep that fee. Kudos to them for that.

reviewDescription:
SHORT: These guys are AWESOME! Fastest and smoothest Refi we've ever done! (our 4th refi in 18yrs)  We knew they were a good company when they honored a mistakenly advertised rate on Bankrate.com.
TLDR: While browsing rates on Bankrate.com last June I saw a 2.25% w/ 0 Points/15yr so decided it was time to refi from our 30yr 3.25%.  Did some googling and after a very quick online application late on Saturday, 20 Jun (yes they work weekends), we were pre-approved by 6am the next morning (Sunday)!  But then I saw that there were 2 discount pts added to the quote. :(  At first I thought this was a bait-n-switch, but they had great reviews online so I asked about the points and showed them the "0 points" advertised rate on Bankrate...well they quickly replied honoring the advertised rate w/ 0 points! Unfortunately, I waited a little too long to lock which caused the points to creep up a bit but we still locked on 23 Jun at 2.25%/15yr with only .19% points! (yes POINT 19!).  It was only a 30day lock but I had all our paperwork ready to upload and was confident we wouldn't be any hold up. Fortunately the whole online experience was very streamlined, went super-fast and they were ready to close on 8 July, only 15 days after lock! That's pretty fast compared to our others!  They also refunded the $500 appraisal fee because it wasn't needed.  I recommend you have your income & employment verification info ready to upload right when you apply and also setup your IRS login accounts in advance. (My IRS account creation had a phone# verification glitch but thankfully Better.com allowed that to slide and used our joint tax return as the additional verification.)  We cannot be happier with how smooth this refi went! Thanks Better.com
reviewCompanyResponse: Hi Mark, thank you so much for your honest review. We are thrilled to hear about your Better experience and look forward to working with you again in the future!

reviewDescription:
Earlier this year a recruiter from better contacted me and that's how I heard them. I did some research on Google for customer reviews. I had the impression that better mortgage is fast in loan processing and 100% online. It was attracting for me at that time since I don't want to go places due to COVID-19.

I started online application on May 8th. I finish all tasks in several hours. On May 19th I received an email stating that the loan is conditionally approved. The loan processor asked me whether to go with the CEMA option and I said yes. She said it could take five weeks. Five weeks later I still received no update so I sent an email. She replied  "I will let you know when I have an update from attorney". Two days later I asked if she had an update from the attorney she said no. Then I asked why can't you just call the attorney to get an update? One day later she said it will be ready in a couple of days.

Another week passed without update. I send an email asking for a phone call since I have some questions to ask. No reply. I tried several times still no reply. I called the phone number on my loan page then I just realized that all those phone numbers just direct to customer service. During that week I called customer service for about 10 times. Each time the response was the same: She was in a meeting and will get back to you later. I also ask customer service to tell her manager and manager's manager. Customer service did that. But still no response in any form. Not even from her manager. I gave up finally since even if her managers don't care what can I do?

After that all the email I received was

"Thank you for your patience."

That sounds like from a reply from customer service but no that's from my loan processor and all she said to me.

I failed to get any phone call for three months and she was always in a meeting. They are not busy for sure. They work 8 hours a day and my loan processor took two long vacations during that three months. Without caring about the work they have done. I know right now a lot of loan originators are super busy in New York but I really don't understand how do they manage to take so many vacations.

Then on Aug 4th I finally received an email from her saying that she will move the case to closing team despite a lot of her unfinished tasks. Two weeks later I am asked to choose a closing date. I chose Aug 25th. At 7pm before that day I received a phone call telling me that they cannot make it since they didn't finish their tasks. Then I rescheduled it to Sept 1st. On 7pm August 31st I received a phone call from them again with the same excuse. He also said that my appraisal is expired.

I don't know how could he say that at ease. It expired only because of their incompetence and I don't know why they just tell me that several hours before closing.

If you are considering a loan through better mortgage you'd better look elsewhere. It's important to work with a human. At better.com once you start you are completely cutoff. Don't even hope to get timely updates. It's a complete blackbox to you.

Also, fast processing is just a shameless lie. I worked with several other banks and closed two of them before, their processing time is about 2-4 times faster than better.com. More importantly there is a real human I can talk to.

Some other things I need to mention:

A lot of the closing cost including rate of points increased in the initial closing disclosure. Some items even increase several times in amount. I don't why they can still increase them after rate lock down and they refuse to give explanation.

They said their rate is lowest and they will match lower rates. I really doubt that. My locked rate at better was 3% + 1.47% points. A month later I asked two other banks I worked before, one of them is 3% with no points and another is 2.99% with no points. Since the quotes are not at the same time I am not exactly sure.
reviewCompanyResponse: Hi there, we apologize for the miscommunication you experienced with us.
